title: Documentation
text:
1 Chapters
BCFNGS-project-management is a Shiny app for a project management system for the BCF NGS core facility.
The NGS lab of the Core Facility provides sequencing as a service. We recommend contacting us before the start of your experiment. In collaboration with the Bioinformatics Core Facility, we provide the following services.
If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to contact us @ omicsdesk!

title: Documentation
text:
1.1 How to render
quarto render Documentation/
This booklet consolidates all operational and technical documentation for the sequencing Shiny app.

title: 2  Quick Start
text:
2.1.1 Contents

Local dev — local mode
Local dev — LDAP simulation
Local dev — unset
VM — LDAP mode (production)
VM — local mode (maintenance)
VM — unset (clear overrides)


2.1.1.1 Local dev — local mode
Sys.setenv(APP_ENV = "dev", AUTH_MODE = "local")
shiny::runApp("sequencing-app")


2.1.1.2 Local dev — LDAP simulation
Sys.setenv(
  APP_ENV = "dev",
  AUTH_MODE = "ldap",
  DEV_REMOTE_USER = "your_username",
  LDAP_URI = "ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv2.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv3.biochem.mpg.de:636",
  LDAP_BASE_DN = "dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de"
)
shiny::runApp("sequencing-app")
To run this locally, replace “your_username” with actual user name.


2.1.1.3 Local dev — unset
Sys.unsetenv(c(
  "APP_ENV","AUTH_MODE","DEV_REMOTE_USER",
  "LDAP_BIND_DN","LDAP_BIND_PW",
  "LDAP_URI","LDAP_BASE_DN","LDAP_DEBUG"
))


2.1.1.4 VM — LDAP mode (production)
sudo systemctl edit shiny-server
[Service]
Environment=AUTH_MODE=ldap
Environment=LDAP_URI=ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv2.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv3.biochem.mpg.de:636
Environment=LDAP_BASE_DN=dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server


2.1.1.5 VM — local mode (maintenance)
[Service]
Environment=AUTH_MODE=local
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server


2.1.1.6 VM — unset (clear overrides)
sudo systemctl edit shiny-server
# remove AUTH_MODE/LDAP_* lines, save, then:
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server

3.3 Modify Text (Announcements)
The post-login landing text is now admin-editable in the app UI (no code edit needed for content changes):

Log in as admin.
Open Administer Projects -> Manage Landing Text.
Select the panel:

Announcement
service_info (service/instructions panel)

Add/Edit/Delete/Reorder white text boxes.
Changes are published immediately and stored in SQLite.


3.3.1 Markdown-lite syntax
Use these formats in each box:

Bold: **important text**
Bullet list:

- first item
- second item

Numbered list:

1. first
2. second

Link: [@NGS](mailto:ngs@biochem.mpg.de)



3.3.2 Where it is stored

DB tables:

announcement_panels
announcement_items

App render + admin handlers:

/Users/yeroslaviz/Documents/Github/BCFNGS-project-management/sequencing-app/app.R

Style classes (unchanged):

.announcement-panel
.service-panel
.info-box


If tables are missing, the app auto-creates them and seeds default content on startup.


3.3.3 SQL handling (moved)
All SQL database commands and explanations were moved to notes/sql-handling.qmd.
This includes:

DB rebuild procedure
Admin user promotion in LDAP mode
Budget holder updates (CSV vs DB)
SQLite inspection and maintenance commands ### Install required system dependencies


sudo apt install -y libssl-dev libxml2-dev libcurl4-openssl-dev libudunits2-dev libgdal-dev



3.3.4 Changing hostname to fit the apache2 config file

# Change the hostname
sudo hostnamectl set-hostname ngs-testing-vm.biochem.mpg.de

# Verify the change
hostnamectl status

nslookup ngs-testing-vm.biochem.mpg.de
ping ngs-testing-vm.biochem.mpg.de
Server:     127.0.0.53

Non-authoritative answer:

64 bytes from ngs-testing-vm.biochem.mpg.de (10.33.0.60): icmp_seq=1 ttl=64 time=0.079 ms
64 bytes from ngs-testing-vm.biochem.mpg.de (10.33.0.60): icmp_seq=2 ttl=64 time=0.074 ms

sudo systemctl restart apache2

title: 3  Setup & Infrastructure
section: 3.5 Ubuntu Bare Server Setup HOWTO (Shiny + LDAP)
text:
3.5 Ubuntu Bare Server Setup HOWTO (Shiny + LDAP)

3.5.1 Restart communication (quick resume)
If I ever need to resume this work with a new AI tool or a new chat session:

Read Troubleshooting for common failures and fixes.
Read Deploy & Operations for the current deploy/validation steps.
Read Change Log for the latest configuration changes and decisions.



3.5.2 Base OS prep
sudo apt update
sudo apt upgrade -y
Install common tools and libraries:
sudo apt install -y \
  git rsync curl ca-certificates \
  build-essential \
  libcurl4-openssl-dev libssl-dev libxml2-dev \
  sqlite3 libsqlite3-dev \
  ldap-utils \
  apache2 apache2-utils
If mail sending via mailR is needed, install a Java runtime:
sudo apt install -y default-jre
If LDAP-related R packages need OpenLDAP headers, install:
sudo apt install -y libldap2-dev libsasl2-dev


3.5.3 Install R
sudo apt install -y r-base
If you need a newer R version than the Ubuntu default, add the CRAN repo and install from there (optional).


3.5.4 Install Shiny Server
Download the latest Shiny Server .deb from Posit and install it:
# Example pattern (replace filename with the current version)
# wget https://download3.rstudio.org/ubuntu-22.04/x86_64/shiny-server-<version>.deb
# sudo dpkg -i shiny-server-<version>.deb
# sudo apt -f install -y
Start and enable the service:
sudo systemctl enable shiny-server
sudo systemctl start shiny-server


3.5.5 Clone the repo
cd /home/<your-user>
git clone git@github.com:yeroslaviz/BCFNGS-project-management.git


3.5.6 Configure the Shiny app folder
sudo usermod -a -G shiny $USER
sudo mv /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app.backup 2>/dev/null || true
sudo mkdir -p /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app
sudo chown -R $USER:shiny /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app
Deploy the app files:
cd /home/<your-user>/BCFNGS-project-management
./scripts/deploy.sh


3.5.7 Install required R packages
From R (or R -q -e):
install.packages(c(
  "shiny",
  "shinyjs",
  "DBI",
  "RSQLite",
  "digest",
  "DT",
  "shinyWidgets",
  "mailR",
  "ldapr"
))
If any package fails, install missing system libraries and retry.


3.5.8 Configure Shiny Server environment variables
Recommended: systemd drop-in (production-safe).
sudo systemctl edit shiny-server
Add:
[Service]
Environment=AUTH_MODE=ldap
Environment=LDAP_URI=ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv2.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv3.biochem.mpg.de:636
Environment=LDAP_BASE_DN=dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de
# Optional debug (dev only):
# Environment=APP_ENV=dev
# Environment=LDAP_DEBUG=1
Then restart:
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
Alternative: /home/shiny/.Renviron (simpler, less explicit).


3.5.9 Configure LDAP trust (OpenLDAP client)
Edit /etc/ldap/ldap.conf and ensure:
BASE            dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de
URI             ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de ldaps://ldapserv2.biochem.mpg.de ldaps://ldapserv3.biochem.mpg.de
TLS_REQCERT     hard
TLS_CACERTDIR   /etc/ssl/certs
Test:
ldapsearch -x -H ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de:636 \
  -b "dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de" "(uid=USER)" mail


3.5.10 Configure Apache reverse proxy + LDAP auth
Copy the repo Apache config and enable required modules:
sudo cp /home/<your-user>/BCFNGS-project-management/mpiseq2/ngs-vm_01-main.conf /etc/apache2/sites-enabled/01-main.conf
sudo a2enmod proxy proxy_http proxy_wstunnel rewrite ssl headers ldap authnz_ldap
sudo apache2ctl -t
sudo systemctl restart apache2


3.5.11 Validate the full flow
curl -Ik https://ngs-testing-vm.biochem.mpg.de/
# Expect 401

curl -Ik -u USER https://ngs-testing-vm.biochem.mpg.de/
# Expect 302 to /sequencing-app/?auth_user=USER

curl -Ik -u USER https://ngs-testing-vm.biochem.mpg.de/sequencing-app/
# Expect 200

curl -I http://localhost:3838/sequencing-app/
# Expect 200


3.5.12 Routine updates
cd /home/<your-user>/BCFNGS-project-management
git pull origin main
./scripts/deploy.sh


3.5.13 Production hygiene

Use a service bind account if LDAP requires bind credentials.
Keep DB files out of git.
Keep Apache debug logs off unless diagnosing a problem.



3.5.14 References

notes/vm-troubleshooting-guide.md
Server_preparations.qmd

title: 3  Setup & Infrastructure
text:
3.7 SSL Certificate (for HTTPS)
the new setting of the certificates. I have three files.
The private key I created when applying for the certificate. The other two were downloaded, when the emial came from Harica (the certificate provider).
the PEM is the pem file and the DER (issuer) is the chain file. Both of them are needed.
Setting for the readability of the files:
cd /etc/apache2/certificate/2026
-rw------- 1 yeroslaviz b_mcf 3272 Nov 10 15:58 private-key.pem # udo chmod 600 /path/to/private.key  # Readable only by root
-rw-r--r-- 1 yeroslaviz b_mcf 1545 Nov 14 12:12 NGS_2026_Issuer_ChainFile.cer # sudo chmod 644 /path/to/chain.crt  # Readable by all
-rw-r--r-- 1 yeroslaviz b_mcf 2796 Nov 14 12:34 NGS_2026_Cert.pem # sudo chmod 644 /path/to/certificate.crt # Readable by all
All the files are saved under /etc/apache2/certificate/2026.
Now I created soft links for each of these files.
sudo ln -s /etc/apache2/certificate/2026/private-key.pem /etc/ssl/private/2026_private-key.pem
sudo ln -s /etc/apache2/certificate/2026/NGS_2026_Cert.pem /etc/ssl/certs/2026_NGS_Cert.pem

title: 4  VM Installation Guide (ngs-vm & bcf-vm)
text:
4.2 Scope
This guide covers a full installation of the Shiny app on two Ubuntu 24 VMs:

bcf-vm: clean install, no HTTPS cert (self-signed OK for testing).
ngs-vm: already running the legacy Perl system; install the Shiny app in parallel, then redirect the public hostname when ready.

title: 4  VM Installation Guide (ngs-vm & bcf-vm)
text:
4.3 Port strategy (recommended)
Recommended for parallel operation on ngs-vm:

Shiny Server stays on :3838 (default).
Apache vhost for the new system listens on :8443 (HTTPS) or another high port (e.g. :1234 if you prefer).
Existing legacy system remains on :443.

Why 8443: it is standard for HTTPS on alternate ports and avoids conflicts.

title: 4  VM Installation Guide (ngs-vm & bcf-vm)
text:
4.4 Prerequisites (both VMs)
Run the requirement check script:
bash /Users/yeroslaviz/Documents/Github/BCFNGS-project-management/scripts/check_requirements.sh
Install missing apt packages:
bash /Users/yeroslaviz/Documents/Github/BCFNGS-project-management/scripts/check_requirements.sh --install
Install missing R packages (including rJava):
bash /Users/yeroslaviz/Documents/Github/BCFNGS-project-management/scripts/check_requirements.sh --install-r
The requirement check reports missing rJava/mailR and missing Java tools (java, javac) explicitly.
Manual steps still required after package install:

Install Shiny Server from the .deb package (see bcf-vm Step 2).
Install Java + R mail dependencies for email notifications (see bcf-vm Step 2b).
Install LDAP client/build dependencies + ldapr (see bcf-vm Step 2d).
Ensure the shiny user exists and app-folder permissions are correct (see bcf-vm Step 2c).
Configure Apache vhost, LDAP, and systemd environment (see bcf-vm Step 6, bcf-vm Step 7, and ngs-vm Step 2).

title: 4  VM Installation Guide (ngs-vm & bcf-vm)
section: 4.5 GitHub connection on a fresh VM
text:
4.5 GitHub connection on a fresh VM
Before cloning on either VM, configure GitHub access.
Use these sections in Git & GitHub:

GitHub connection on VM (clone/pull/push)
Enable push from the VM
Repo setup on the VM

Minimum check before clone:
ssh -T git@github.com
If this fails, do not continue with clone/deploy until SSH access is fixed.

title: 4  VM Installation Guide (ngs-vm & bcf-vm)
text:
4.6 bcf-vm: clean install

4.6.1 1) Install base packages
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install -y git curl rsync apache2 apache2-utils openssl sqlite3 ldap-utils \
  build-essential libcurl4-openssl-dev libssl-dev libxml2-dev r-base


4.6.2 2) Install Shiny Server
Copy the .deb to the VM and install it:
sudo apt-get install -y gdebi-core
wget https://download3.rstudio.org/ubuntu-20.04/x86_64/shiny-server-1.5.23.1030-amd64.deb
sudo gdebi shiny-server-1.5.23.1030-amd64.deb


4.6.3 2b) Configure Java + mail packages (required for email notifications)
The app sends emails via mailR, which requires Java / rJava. This is for email notifications, not for LDAP auth itself.
sudo apt-get install -y default-jdk
sudo R CMD javareconf
sudo Rscript --vanilla -e 'install.packages(c("rJava","mailR"), repos="https://cloud.r-project.org")'
Apply the same on ngs-vm and bcf-vm.


4.6.4 2c) Create shiny user + app-folder permissions
Shiny Server runs the app as the shiny service user.
getent passwd shiny || sudo useradd --system --home /var/lib/shiny-server --shell /usr/sbin/nologin shiny
sudo mkdir -p /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app
sudo chown -R shiny:shiny /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app
sudo chmod 755 /srv/shiny-server /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app
sudo usermod -a -G shiny "$USER"
If you are configuring a different account, replace "$USER" with that username.


4.6.5 2d) LDAP packages (Ubuntu + R)
Install LDAP client tools and build dependencies:
sudo apt-get install -y ldap-utils libldap2-dev libsasl2-dev ca-certificates
Configure OpenLDAP client trust in /etc/ldap/ldap.conf:
sudo tee /etc/ldap/ldap.conf >/dev/null <<'EOF'
BASE            dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de
URI             ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de ldaps://ldapserv2.biochem.mpg.de ldaps://ldapserv3.biochem.mpg.de
TLS_REQCERT     hard
TLS_CACERTDIR   /etc/ssl/certs
EOF
Install the R LDAP package:
sudo Rscript --vanilla -e 'install.packages("ldapr", repos="https://cloud.r-project.org")'
Fallback if CRAN install is unavailable on this VM:
sudo Rscript --vanilla -e 'if (!requireNamespace("remotes", quietly=TRUE)) install.packages("remotes", repos="https://cloud.r-project.org"); remotes::install_github("liamgilbey/ldapr")'
Quick checks:
ldapsearch -x -H ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de:636 -b "dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de" "(uid=<user>)" mail
sudo -u shiny Rscript --vanilla -e 'library(ldapr); cat("ldapr OK\n")'
If ldapsearch only works with LDAPTLS_REQCERT=never, your /etc/ldap/ldap.conf trust settings are incomplete or incorrect.


4.6.6 3) Create a self-signed certificate (test only)
sudo mkdir -p /etc/ssl/localcerts
sudo openssl req -x509 -nodes -days 365 \
  -newkey rsa:2048 \
  -keyout /etc/ssl/localcerts/bcf-vm.key \
  -out /etc/ssl/localcerts/bcf-vm.crt


4.6.7 4) Clone repo and set app folder
cd /home/<user>
git clone git@github.com:yeroslaviz/BCFNGS-project-management.git
sudo rsync -av /home/<user>/BCFNGS-project-management/sequencing-app/ /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/
sudo chown -R shiny:shiny /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app


4.6.8 5) Initialize the SQLite DB
cd /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app
sudo -u shiny Rscript setup_database.R


4.6.9 6) Configure LDAP mode (systemd override)
sudo systemctl edit shiny-server
[Service]
Environment=AUTH_MODE=ldap
Environment=LDAP_URI=ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv2.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv3.biochem.mpg.de:636
Environment=LDAP_BASE_DN=dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de
Environment=TRUST_PROXY_AUTH_USER_QUERY=1
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server


4.6.10 6b) Ensure app runtime env is loaded (.Renviron)
On bcf-vm and ngs-vm, we observed that LDAP worked reliably only after setting app env in:

/srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/.Renviron

Create/update this file:
sudo tee /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/.Renviron >/dev/null <<'EOF'
AUTH_MODE=ldap
LDAP_URI=ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv2.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv3.biochem.mpg.de:636
LDAP_BASE_DN=dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de
TRUST_PROXY_AUTH_USER_QUERY=1
# LDAP_DEBUG=1   # only while debugging
EOF
sudo chown shiny:shiny /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/.Renviron
sudo chmod 640 /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/.Renviron
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
This prevents the app from falling back to local-mode UI when systemd env is present but not visible inside the R worker.


4.6.11 7) Configure Apache (test HTTPS redirect)
Use the versioned template from the repo:

scripts/bcf.vm.conf

Copy it on VM:
sudo cp /home/<user>/BCFNGS-project-management/scripts/bcf.vm.conf /etc/apache2/sites-available/bcf-vm.conf
Template includes:

HTTP→HTTPS redirect (:80 → :443)
Scoped proxy to /sequencing-app/
LDAP <Location /sequencing-app/> auth block
Canonical auth_user rewrite and websocket rule

Enable the site and restart Apache:
sudo a2enmod ssl proxy proxy_http proxy_wstunnel headers rewrite ldap authnz_ldap
sudo a2ensite bcf-vm.conf # this creates symlink to sites-enabled
sudo apache2ctl -t
sudo systemctl restart apache2

title: 4  VM Installation Guide (ngs-vm & bcf-vm)
section: 4.7 ngs-vm: parallel install next to perl system
text:
4.7 ngs-vm: parallel install next to perl system

4.7.1 1) Keep legacy system on :443
Leave the currently active Perl :443 site enabled (on our VM this is 02-testing.conf).


4.7.2 2) Add a parallel Shiny vhost on :8443 (or :1234)
Use the versioned template from the repo:

scripts/ngs-vm-shiny.conf

Copy it on VM:
sudo cp /home/<user>/BCFNGS-project-management/scripts/ngs-vm-shiny.conf /etc/apache2/sites-available/ngs-vm-shiny.conf
Keep certificate paths unchanged in this file:

SSLCertificateFile /etc/ssl/certs/ngs-cert.pem
SSLCertificateKeyFile /etc/ssl/private/2026_private-key.pem
SSLCertificateChainFile /etc/apache2/certificate/2026_Cert_binary.cer

Enable and reload:
grep -q '^Listen 8443$' /etc/apache2/ports.conf || echo 'Listen 8443' | sudo tee -a /etc/apache2/ports.conf
sudo a2enmod ssl proxy proxy_http headers rewrite ldap authnz_ldap
sudo a2ensite ngs-vm-shiny.conf
sudo apache2ctl -t
sudo systemctl restart apache2


4.7.3 3) Validate parallel access
curl -Ik https://ngs-vm.biochem.mpg.de:8443/
curl -Ik -u USER https://ngs-vm.biochem.mpg.de:8443/sequencing-app/
If hostname/port tests fail, validate locally against the vhost:
curl -kI -H 'Host: ngs-vm.biochem.mpg.de' https://127.0.0.1:8443/
curl -kI -u USER -H 'Host: ngs-vm.biochem.mpg.de' https://127.0.0.1:8443/sequencing-app/
If the app returns HTTP 500 with mailR/rJava errors on ngs-vm, apply this runtime fix:
sudo apt-get install -y openjdk-17-jdk r-cran-rjava
export JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64
sudo env JAVA_HOME="$JAVA_HOME" /usr/bin/R CMD javareconf
sudo env JAVA_HOME="$JAVA_HOME" /usr/bin/Rscript --vanilla -e 'install.packages("mailR", repos="https://cloud.r-project.org", INSTALL_opts="--no-test-load")'
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server


4.7.4 4) Switch between LDAP and local modes on VM
Primary reference: LDAP chapter - switch between LDAP and local modes
In production, switch modes by editing:

/srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/.Renviron

Set LDAP mode:
sudo sed -i 's/^AUTH_MODE=.*/AUTH_MODE=ldap/' /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/.Renviron
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
Set local mode:
sudo sed -i 's/^AUTH_MODE=.*/AUTH_MODE=local/' /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/.Renviron
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
Verify effective runtime mode from app logs:
LATEST=$(sudo ls -1t /var/log/shiny-server/sequencing-app-shiny-*.log | head -n1)
sudo tail -n 80 "$LATEST"

title: 4  VM Installation Guide (ngs-vm & bcf-vm)
text:
4.8 Legacy data migration (perl-VM → shiny-VM)
Use this workflow to export legacy projects from the perl system on ngs-vm and import into either bcf-vm or ngs-vm (Shiny DB).

4.8.1 Step 1) Export CSV on ngs-vm (perl PostgreSQL)
psql -U letunic -h 127.0.0.1 -d mpiseq2_db -c "\
\\copy (\
  SELECT \
    p.id AS legacy_id, \
    p.name AS project_name, \
    m.login AS login, \
    m.name AS user_full_name, \
    l.name AS group_name, \
    COALESCE(p.ref_genome_legacy, rg.genome) AS reference_genome, \
    COALESCE(p.sample_type_legacy, st.type) AS sample_type, \
    t.type AS type_label, \
    p.sequencing_length AS sequencing_length, \
    COALESCE(p.budget_group_legacy, bg.budget) AS budget_group, \
    p.sequencing_platform AS sequencing_platform, \
    COALESCE(p.sequencing_kit_legacy, p.sequencing_kit) AS sequencing_kit, \
    p.sequencing_index AS sequencing_index, \
    p.note AS note \
  FROM project.project p \
  LEFT JOIN people.member m ON p.user_id = m.id \
  LEFT JOIN people.lab l ON m.lab_id = l.id \
  LEFT JOIN project.ref_genome rg ON p.ref_genome_id = rg.id \
  LEFT JOIN project.sample_type st ON p.sample_type_id = st.id \
  LEFT JOIN project.type t ON p.type_id = t.id \
  LEFT JOIN project.budget_group bg ON p.budget_group_id = bg.id \
  ORDER BY p.id\
) TO '/www/mpiseq2/exports/legacy_projects.csv' CSV HEADER"


4.8.2 Step 2) Normalize UTF-8 (recommended)
sudo python3 - <<'PY'
src = "/www/mpiseq2/exports/legacy_projects.csv"
dst = "/www/mpiseq2/exports/legacy_projects.utf8.csv"
with open(src, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f, \
     open(dst, "w", encoding="utf-8") as out:
    for line in f:
        if "Ã" in line or "Â" in line:
            line = line.encode("latin1", errors="ignore").decode("utf-8", errors="ignore")
        out.write(line)
PY


4.8.3 Step 3) Move CSV to the target VM
If importing into bcf-vm:
# run on bcf-vm once
mkdir -p /home/yeroslaviz/BCFNGS-project-management/exports/

# run on ngs-vm (source)
scp /www/mpiseq2/exports/legacy_projects.utf8.csv \
  yeroslaviz@bcf-vm:/home/yeroslaviz/BCFNGS-project-management/exports/
If importing into Shiny on ngs-vm (same host as perl system):
cp -R /www/mpiseq2/exports/ /home/yeroslaviz/BCFNGS-project-management/


4.8.4 Step 4) Import into Shiny SQLite DB
sudo systemctl stop shiny-server
cd /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app
If /home/yeroslaviz is not traversable by shiny (permission denied), either:

quick fix:

sudo chmod o+x /home/yeroslaviz

safer fix (preferred): copy script + CSV to a service-owned path and run from there.

Then run import:
sudo -u shiny python3 /home/yeroslaviz/BCFNGS-project-management/scripts/import_legacy_projects.py \
  --db /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db \
  --csv /home/yeroslaviz/BCFNGS-project-management/exports/legacy_projects.utf8.csv

sudo systemctl start shiny-server


4.8.5 Step 5) Verify import
sudo -u shiny sqlite3 /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db \
"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM projects WHERE status='Legacy project';"

sudo -u shiny sqlite3 /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db \
"SELECT project_id, project_name, responsible_user, status FROM projects WHERE status='Legacy project' ORDER BY project_id LIMIT 5;"
Notes:

Rebuild is not required for regular imports; new legacy_id rows are inserted, already imported IDs are skipped.
If you need a full refresh of legacy rows, rebuild or delete legacy rows first, then re-import.
Detailed SQL notes remain in Documentation/chapters/sql-handling.qmd.

title: 4  VM Installation Guide (ngs-vm & bcf-vm)
text:
4.9 Cutover and rollback

4.9.1 Cutover target
After cutover:

https://ngs-vm.biochem.mpg.de serves Shiny on :443 (no port in URL).
Perl fallback stays available on https://ngs-vm.biochem.mpg.de:9443.



4.9.2 1) Pre-cutover safety backup
sudo mkdir -p /root/apache-backup-$(date +%F-%H%M)
sudo cp -a /etc/apache2/sites-available /root/apache-backup-$(date +%F-%H%M)/
sudo cp -a /etc/apache2/sites-enabled /root/apache-backup-$(date +%F-%H%M)/


4.9.3 2) Prepare vhost files
Use the versioned templates from the repo:

scripts/ngs-vm-shiny-443.conf -> /etc/apache2/sites-available/ngs-vm-shiny-443.conf
scripts/ngs-vm-perl-9443.conf -> /etc/apache2/sites-available/ngs-vm-perl-9443.conf

sudo cp /home/<user>/BCFNGS-project-management/scripts/ngs-vm-shiny-443.conf /etc/apache2/sites-available/ngs-vm-shiny-443.conf
sudo cp /home/<user>/BCFNGS-project-management/scripts/ngs-vm-perl-9443.conf /etc/apache2/sites-available/ngs-vm-perl-9443.conf


4.9.4 3) Enable the cutover routing
grep -q '^Listen 9443$' /etc/apache2/ports.conf || echo 'Listen 9443' | sudo tee -a /etc/apache2/ports.conf
sudo a2enmod ssl proxy proxy_http proxy_wstunnel rewrite headers ldap authnz_ldap
sudo a2ensite ngs-vm-shiny-443.conf
sudo a2ensite ngs-vm-perl-9443.conf
# Disable the old Perl :443 site (name can vary by VM):
sudo a2dissite 02-testing.conf || true
sudo a2dissite default-ssl.conf || true
sudo apache2ctl -t
sudo systemctl restart apache2


4.9.5 4) Automated switch/rollback script (optional)
You can use:

scripts/cutover_ngs_vm_ports.sh

Cutover:
cd /home/<user>/BCFNGS-project-management
./scripts/cutover_ngs_vm_ports.sh cutover
cutover auto-disables competing :443 sites (including default-ssl.conf when present).
Rollback:
cd /home/<user>/BCFNGS-project-management
./scripts/cutover_ngs_vm_ports.sh rollback
rollback re-enables the first available legacy :443 site in this order:

PERL_443_SITE (if provided)
02-testing.conf
default-ssl.conf



4.9.6 Rollback (manual)
sudo a2ensite 02-testing.conf
sudo a2dissite ngs-vm-shiny-443.conf
sudo a2dissite ngs-vm-perl-9443.conf
sudo apache2ctl -t
sudo systemctl restart apache2


4.9.7 DB lifecycle policy (important)

scripts/deploy.sh is code-only deployment and does not recreate sequencing_projects.db.
Restarting shiny-server does not delete DB content.
Rebuild is manual and only when intentionally running /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/setup_database.R.
Keep legacy CSV import as a separate operation, not part of deploy.



4.9.8 DB snapshot / rebuild / restore runbook
Snapshot:
sudo systemctl stop shiny-server
sudo cp /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db.bak.$(date +%F-%H%M%S)
sudo systemctl start shiny-server
Optional rebuild:
cd /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app
sudo systemctl stop shiny-server
sudo -u shiny Rscript setup_database.R
sudo systemctl start shiny-server
Restore snapshot:
sudo systemctl stop shiny-server
sudo cp /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db.bak.<timestamp> /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db
sudo chown shiny:shiny /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db
sudo systemctl start shiny-server

title: 5  Deploy & Operations
text:
5.3 Deploy Workflow

5.3.1 Production rollout checklist (quick reference)
This is the short checklist I use on production. Details for each step are below in the VM rollout HOW‑TO section.

Confirm GitHub access and pull latest code.

ssh -T git@github.com
cd /home/yeroslaviz/BCFNGS-project-management
git pull origin main

Take backups (app folder, SQLite DB, Apache config).

sudo rsync -av /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/ /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app.backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M)/
cp /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db \
  /srv/shiny-server/sequencing_projects.db.backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M)
sudo cp /etc/apache2/sites-enabled/01-main.conf \
  /etc/apache2/sites-enabled/01-main.conf.backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M)

Deploy.

./scripts/deploy.sh

Restart services.

sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
sudo systemctl restart apache2

Smoke‑test login and app behavior.

curl -Ik https://ngs-testing-vm.biochem.mpg.de/
curl -Ik -u USER https://ngs-testing-vm.biochem.mpg.de/
curl -Ik -u USER https://ngs-testing-vm.biochem.mpg.de/sequencing-app/

Roll back from backups if needed.

sudo rsync -av --delete /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app.backup_YYYYMMDD_HHMM/ /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
sudo systemctl restart apache2


5.3.2 VM rollout HOW‑TO (Git + deploy + safety)
This is the repeatable checklist I use when rolling out to a VM (first ngs-testing-vm, then ngs-vm).

5.3.2.1 Step 1: Make sure GitHub access works on the VM
Check which remote is set:
git -C /home/yeroslaviz/BCFNGS-project-management remote -v
If the remote is SSH (git@github.com:...), verify SSH access:
ssh -T git@github.com
If SSH works but git pull still says Permission denied (publickey), the VM key is not added to the repo. The fix is to add the VM public key as a Deploy Key:
cat ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub
Then add that key in GitHub: Repo → Settings → Deploy keys → Add deploy key (read‑only is enough for pull).


5.3.2.2 Step 2: Pull latest code
cd /home/yeroslaviz/BCFNGS-project-management
git pull origin main
If Git says divergent branches, I set merge as the default and pull again:
git config pull.rebase false
git pull origin main
If Git refuses because of the local SQLite DB file, I stop tracking the DB on the VM:
git rm --cached sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db
git commit -m "Stop tracking local SQLite DB on VM"
git pull origin main
This does not delete the local DB file. It just removes it from Git so future pulls work.


5.3.2.3 Step 3: Production safety backups (fast rollback)
sudo rsync -av /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/ /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app.backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M)/
cp /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db \
  /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db.backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M)
sudo cp /etc/apache2/sites-enabled/01-main.conf \
  /etc/apache2/sites-enabled/01-main.conf.backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M)


5.3.2.4 Step 4: Deploy
./scripts/deploy.sh


5.3.2.5 Step 5: Restart and smoke‑test
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
sudo systemctl restart apache2
Then open the app URL and verify: - LDAP prompt appears - login works - app loads


5.3.2.6 Step 6: Rollback (if needed)
sudo rsync -av --delete /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app.backup_YYYYMMDD_HHMM/ /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
sudo systemctl restart apache2

text:
5.4 Deploy Scope and DB Behavior
scripts/deploy.sh is code deployment only:

syncs app files from repo -> /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/
excludes sequencing_projects.db
restarts shiny-server
runs LDAP smoke tests (unless SKIP_SMOKE_TEST=1)

It does not:

rebuild the DB
import legacy CSV
run setup_database.R
overwrite landing text content (announcement_panels / announcement_items)


5.4.1 When to run deploy
Run deploy after changes to app code/config in:

/home/yeroslaviz/BCFNGS-project-management/sequencing-app/

Do not use deploy for Apache-only changes; apply Apache config separately.


5.4.2 Restart vs rebuild

Restarting shiny-server does not remove DB content.
Rebuild is a manual, explicit action (setup_database.R) and should be done only when needed.
Landing text edited via Manage Landing Text is DB-backed and survives deploy/restart.



5.4.3 Safe DB snapshot around risky maintenance
sudo systemctl stop shiny-server
sudo cp /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db.bak.$(date +%F-%H%M%S)
sudo systemctl start shiny-server
Restore snapshot:
sudo systemctl stop shiny-server
sudo cp /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db.bak.<timestamp> /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db
sudo chown shiny:shiny /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db

5.5 LDAP Deploy Smoke Tests

5.5.1 Step 1: Deploy with built-in smoke tests
Run on VM:
cd ~/BCFNGS-project-management
./scripts/deploy.sh
The script now:

Deploys app files to /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/
Restarts shiny-server
Runs LDAP smoke tests:

/sequencing-app/ returns 302 to canonical ?auth_user=<logged-user>
tampered ?auth_user=other is rewritten to ?auth_user=<logged-user>
final app load returns 200


It exits with non-zero status and prints remediation hints if any check fails.

title: 5  Deploy & Operations
text:
5.6 Backups (summary)
This project currently has three backup mechanisms:

Manual pre-deploy rollback backups (recommended before any deploy/maintenance): copy the app folder, the SQLite DB, and the Apache vhost config with a timestamp. See “Production safety backups (fast rollback)” above.
Manual in-app backup/restore (Admin UI): admins can download a .sqlite backup and restore it later (restore makes a pre-restore copy first).
Automatic bi-weekly backups: present in the app code but commented out / disabled by default.

Note:

scripts/deploy.sh syncs app code only. If you changed Apache config, apply/restart Apache separately, then run curl checks.


5.6.1 Step 2: Non-interactive usage (optional)
If you do not want password prompts:
LDAP_TEST_USER="yeroslaviz-test" LDAP_TEST_PASSWORD="YOUR_PASSWORD" ./scripts/deploy.sh
Skip smoke tests only when needed:
SKIP_SMOKE_TEST=1 ./scripts/deploy.sh
Override target base URL:
PUBLIC_BASE_URL="https://ngs-vm.biochem.mpg.de" ./scripts/deploy.sh

title: 5  Deploy & Operations
text:
5.7 Feature Branch Deploy (Test VM)
Use this when you want to validate a feature branch on bcf-vm or ngs-testing-vm before merging to main.

Switch to your feature branch on the VM and pull latest branch commits.

cd /home/<user>/BCFNGS-project-management
git fetch origin
git checkout <feature-branch>
git pull --ff-only origin <feature-branch>

Deploy branch code to /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/.

PUBLIC_BASE_URL="https://<vm-hostname>" ./scripts/deploy.sh

Validate app behavior on that VM (LDAP/local mode, forms, emails, admin actions).
After merge to main, switch VM back to main and deploy again:

cd /home/<user>/BCFNGS-project-management
git checkout main
git pull --ff-only origin main
PUBLIC_BASE_URL="https://<vm-hostname>" ./scripts/deploy.sh
Notes:

scripts/deploy.sh does not rebuild or replace the SQLite DB.
DB-backed content (projects, landing text, additional costs) persists across deploy and restart.

title: 6  LDAP Authentication
text:
6.2 Dependencies (Ubuntu + R)
Install LDAP client tools and headers on Ubuntu:
sudo apt-get install -y ldap-utils libldap2-dev libsasl2-dev ca-certificates
Install ldapr in R:
sudo Rscript --vanilla -e 'install.packages("ldapr", repos="https://cloud.r-project.org")'
Fallback if CRAN install is unavailable:
sudo Rscript --vanilla -e 'if (!requireNamespace("remotes", quietly=TRUE)) install.packages("remotes", repos="https://cloud.r-project.org"); remotes::install_github("liamgilbey/ldapr")'
Verify:
ldapsearch -x -H ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de:636 -b "dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de" "(uid=<user>)" mail
sudo -u shiny Rscript --vanilla -e 'library(ldapr); cat("ldapr OK\n")'
Note:

Java / rJava / mailR are for email notifications, not required for LDAP itself.

title: 6  LDAP Authentication
text: 6.3 LDAP Configuration

title: 6  LDAP Authentication
text:
6.4 LDAP
The app uses Apache LDAP authentication in front of Shiny. The Apache config template in this repo is mpiseq2/ngs-vm_01-main.conf.
Enable required Apache modules:
sudo a2enmod ldap authnz_ldap headers
sudo systemctl reload apache2
Test whether anonymous LDAP search works:
ldapsearch -x -H ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de -b "dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de" "(uid=YOURUSER)"
If anonymous search fails, use bind credentials from IT and add them to the Apache config:
AuthLDAPBindDN "cn=binduser,dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de"
AuthLDAPBindPassword "REDACTED"






Warning



For production, do not use a personal account for LDAP binding. Ask IT for a dedicated service bind account with read‑only permissions and store those credentials securely.


Set app environment variables for Shiny Server (systemd override recommended):
sudo systemctl edit shiny-server
[Service]
Environment=AUTH_MODE=ldap
Environment=LDAP_URI=ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv2.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv3.biochem.mpg.de:636
Environment=LDAP_BASE_DN=dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de
# If you want to restrict search to the user OU:
# Environment=LDAP_BASE_DN=ou=People,dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de
# Optional bind credentials (only if anonymous LDAP search is blocked):
# Environment=LDAP_BIND_DN=cn=binduser,dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de
# Environment=LDAP_BIND_PW=REDACTED
Restart services after config changes:
sudo systemctl restart apache2
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server

6.4.1 Admin access in LDAP mode (VM)
Moved to notes/sql-handling.qmd (admin promotion and verification commands).


6.4.2 Switch between LDAP and local modes (VM)
I switch auth modes by changing the AUTH_MODE environment variable for the shiny-server service and restarting it.
Open the systemd override:
sudo systemctl edit shiny-server
LDAP mode (production):
[Service]
Environment=AUTH_MODE=ldap
Environment=LDAP_URI=ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv2.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv3.biochem.mpg.de:636
Environment=LDAP_BASE_DN=dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de
Local mode (maintenance/testing):
[Service]
Environment=AUTH_MODE=local
Apply changes:
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
sudo systemctl show shiny-server --property=Environment
If I switch back to LDAP, I also remove any dev‑only variables like DEV_REMOTE_USER.


6.4.3 Local dev testing (LDAP mode)
To test LDAP integration on your local machine without affecting production, run the app in dev mode and simulate the authenticated user. If LDAP attribute lookup is needed, provide a bind DN/password (personal credentials are OK for local testing only).
Sys.setenv(
  APP_ENV = "dev",
  AUTH_MODE = "ldap",
  DEV_REMOTE_USER = "your_username",
  LDAP_BIND_DN = "uid=your_username,ou=YourGroup,ou=Research Group,ou=People,dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de",
  LDAP_BIND_PW = "YOUR_PASSWORD"
)
shiny::runApp("sequencing-app")
To switch back to normal local login (no LDAP), run:
Sys.setenv(APP_ENV = "dev", AUTH_MODE = "local")
shiny::runApp("sequencing-app")
To fully clear the LDAP/testing variables in your R session:
Sys.unsetenv(c(
  "APP_ENV", "AUTH_MODE", "DEV_REMOTE_USER",
  "LDAP_BIND_DN", "LDAP_BIND_PW",
  "LDAP_URI", "LDAP_BASE_DN", "LDAP_DEBUG"
))


6.4.4 How I run the app locally (my notes)
When I want the normal local login, I run:
Sys.setenv(APP_ENV = "dev", AUTH_MODE = "local")
shiny::runApp("sequencing-app")
When I want to simulate production LDAP login, I run:
Sys.setenv(
  APP_ENV = "dev",
  AUTH_MODE = "ldap",
  DEV_REMOTE_USER = "my_username",
  LDAP_URI = "ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv2.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv3.biochem.mpg.de:636",
  LDAP_BASE_DN = "dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de"
)
shiny::runApp("sequencing-app")
If I want to fully reset the session, I clear the env vars:
Sys.unsetenv(c(
  "APP_ENV", "AUTH_MODE", "DEV_REMOTE_USER",
  "LDAP_BIND_DN", "LDAP_BIND_PW",
  "LDAP_URI", "LDAP_BASE_DN", "LDAP_DEBUG"
))
Local vs LDAP mode (practical difference for me):

Local mode uses the SQLite users/passwords and shows the normal login screen.
LDAP mode auto‑logs in with the LDAP user, pulls email/phone/OU, and hides the local login UI.
LDAP mode needs network access to the institute LDAP servers.

Dev badge: In dev mode, I see a small badge in the app header that shows DEV · LDAP or DEV · LOCAL, so I always know which mode I’m running.


6.4.5 Budget holder auto-assignment (first login)
On first LDAP login, the app attempts to map the user to a budget holder based on the LDAP ou value.
If the ou contains a group key in parentheses (e.g., (... Cox)), the app matches that key against budget_holders and stores the full PI name (e.g., Cox Juergen) in users.research_group.
When a new project is created, this research_group value is used to auto‑select the budget holder (and thus the cost center).
If multiple cost centers exist for the same PI, the first match is selected by default and can be adjusted during project creation.

6.6 LDAP + Project Management App Summary (2026-02-06)

6.6.1 What Changed

Added LDAP login flow with auto-login via X-Remote-User and local dev simulation via DEV_REMOTE_USER.
LDAP attribute lookup now works without a bind account (anonymous bind), and falls back to ldapsearch for older ldapr versions.
Added a dev-only auth toggle and mode badge (DEV · LDAP / DEV · LOCAL).
Mapped LDAP ou values to budget holders, auto-selecting the correct PI in the project form (with partial match fallback).
Added notices when a PI has multiple cost centers or missing cost center.
Added “Other / not listed” budget holder option so users can enter a new PI (name + surname required; cost center optional with warning).
scripts/deploy.sh now uses rsync and excludes the SQLite DB.
Docs updated with LDAP setup, local testing, and env cleanup.
.gitignore updated to ignore DB files and backup folders.

)
shiny::runApp("sequencing-app")
Implications - Auto-login (no local password). - Pulls mail, telephoneNumber, and ou via LDAP. - Auto-creates user in SQLite if missing. - Matches production behavior.


6.6.5 Clear Dev Environment Variables
Sys.unsetenv(c(
  "APP_ENV", "AUTH_MODE", "DEV_REMOTE_USER",
  "LDAP_BIND_DN", "LDAP_BIND_PW",
  "LDAP_URI", "LDAP_BASE_DN", "LDAP_DEBUG"
))


6.6.6 SQL handling (moved)
All SQL database commands and explanations were moved to notes/sql-handling.qmd.


6.6.7 Budget Holder Behavior

On first LDAP login, the app maps LDAP ou to a PI name in budget_holders.
If multiple cost centers exist for a PI, the app displays a notice.
If cost center is missing (N.A./empty), the app displays a warning.
“Other / not listed” allows users to enter a new PI and inserts into budget_holders automatically.



6.6.8 Deploy (VM)
cd /home/yeroslaviz/BCFNGS-project-management
git pull
./scripts/deploy.sh
scripts/deploy.sh uses rsync and does not overwrite the SQLite DB.


6.6.9 LDAP on VM (when ready)

Update Apache config (template in mpiseq2/ngs-vm_01-main.conf).
Enable modules:
sudo a2enmod ldap authnz_ldap headers
sudo systemctl reload apache2
Set Shiny env vars:
sudo systemctl edit shiny-server
[Service]
Environment=AUTH_MODE=ldap
Environment=LDAP_URI=ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv2.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv3.biochem.mpg.de:636
Environment=LDAP_BASE_DN=dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de
# Optional: restrict to People OU
# Environment=LDAP_BASE_DN=ou=People,dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de
Restart services:
sudo systemctl restart apache2
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server



6.6.10 Switching Modes on VM
sudo systemctl edit shiny-server

LDAP mode: Environment=AUTH_MODE=ldap
Local mode: Environment=AUTH_MODE=local (or remove the line)

Restart:
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server


6.6.11 Why DB Files Are Ignored (moved)
See notes/sql-handling.qmd for the full rationale and git commands.


6.6.12 Tools Needed

ldapsearch is required for LDAP attribute fallback:

Ubuntu: sudo apt install -y ldap-utils
macOS: brew install openldap

title: 7  VM Troubleshooting Guide
text:
7.2 Documentation

7.2.1 Book search not working (file://)
Quarto search requires a web server. If you open the docs directly via file://, search is disabled.
Fix (local server):
cd /Users/yeroslaviz/Documents/Github/BCFNGS-project-management/Documentation/_site
python3 -m http.server 8000
Then open:
http://localhost:8000
Alternative:
quarto preview /Users/yeroslaviz/Documents/Github/BCFNGS-project-management/Documentation

title: 7  VM Troubleshooting Guide
text:
7.3 Git / GitHub

7.3.1 Permission denied (publickey)
Symptoms:

git@github.com: Permission denied (publickey)
fatal: Could not read from remote repository

Fix:

Verify SSH auth:

ssh -T git@github.com

Confirm repo URL:

git remote -v

Ensure the correct SSH key is loaded (or add it to ~/.ssh/config).



7.3.2 Divergent branches during pull
Symptoms:
fatal: Need to specify how to reconcile divergent branches.
Fix:
git config pull.rebase false
# or git config pull.rebase true


7.3.3 Local changes block pull
Symptoms:
error: Your local changes ... would be overwritten by merge
Fix:
git stash push -m "vm local changes"
git pull origin main
git stash pop
Resolve conflicts if they appear.


7.3.4 Merge conflict in config files
Fix options:
# Keep repo version
git checkout --ours <file>

# Keep local version
git checkout --theirs <file>


7.3.5 Untracked files in the VM
Symptoms:
?? sequencing-app/test_detailed.R
Fix:

Ignore, delete, or add to .gitignore.

title: 7  VM Troubleshooting Guide
text:
7.4 Apache / LDAP

7.4.1 Apache won’t restart after LDAP change
Symptoms:
AH00526: Syntax error ... Bad LDAP URL while parsing.
Fix:
Use a single, complete LDAP URL:
AuthLDAPURL "ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de:636/dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de?uid?sub?(objectClass=posixAccount)"


7.4.2 Invalid command 'RequestHeader'
Symptoms:
Invalid command 'RequestHeader', perhaps misspelled or defined by a module not included
Fix:
sudo a2enmod headers
sudo apache2ctl -t
sudo systemctl restart apache2


7.4.3 Invalid command LDAPTLS_CACERTDIR
Cause:
LDAPTLS_CACERTDIR is not an Apache directive.
Fix:
Remove from Apache config. Put TLS trust in /etc/ldap/ldap.conf instead.


7.4.4 ldap_simple_bind() failed: Can’t contact LDAP server
Fix:

Check port:

nc -vz ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de 636

Test TLS bypass:

LDAPTLS_REQCERT=never ldapsearch -x -H ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de:636 \
  -b "dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de" "(uid=USER)" mail

Fix /etc/ldap/ldap.conf:

TLS_CACERTDIR /etc/ssl/certs


7.4.5 LDAPTrustedGlobalCert error
Symptoms:
LDAPTrustedGlobalCert cannot occur within <VirtualHost> section
Fix:
Move it to a global Apache config or comment it out (if not needed).


7.4.6 LDAP works, but /sequencing-app/ returns 404
Symptom

curl -Ik -u USER https://host/sequencing-app/ returns 404
curl -I http://localhost:3838/sequencing-app/ returns 200

Cause
Apache is proxying the wrong path (often due to an older ProxyPass / rule or a backup file in sites-enabled).
Fix

Ensure only these lines exist in the active config:

ProxyPass /sequencing-app/ http://localhost:3838/sequencing-app/
ProxyPassReverse /sequencing-app/ http://localhost:3838/sequencing-app/

Remove or move any backup config files out of /etc/apache2/sites-enabled (Apache can accidentally parse them).
Restart Apache:

sudo apache2ctl -t
sudo systemctl restart apache2


7.4.7 ERR_SSL_PROTOCOL_ERROR for https://bcf-vm/
Symptom

Browser shows: This site can’t provide a secure connection / ERR_SSL_PROTOCOL_ERROR
Usually appears while testing the new bcf-vm host over HTTPS.

Common causes

Hostname is not mapped on your local machine (/etc/hosts missing).
Apache default site (000-default.conf) is still active and interferes on :80.
SSL vhost/modules are not fully enabled.

Fix

On your local machine, map VM IP to hostnames:

sudo sh -c 'echo "10.33.0.55 bcf-vm bcf-vm.biochem.mpg.de" >> /etc/hosts'
sudo dscacheutil -flushcache
sudo killall -HUP mDNSResponder

On bcf-vm, keep only the intended site active and enable SSL:

sudo a2dissite 000-default.conf
sudo a2ensite bcf-vm.conf
sudo a2enmod ssl headers rewrite proxy proxy_http
sudo apache2ctl -t
sudo systemctl restart apache2

Verify listeners/vhosts:

sudo ss -ltnp | grep -E ':80|:443'
sudo apache2ctl -S

Validate with curl:

curl -vk https://bcf-vm/
curl -vk https://bcf-vm.biochem.mpg.de/
If needed, test locally on VM with explicit host header:
curl -kI -H 'Host: bcf-vm.biochem.mpg.de' https://127.0.0.1/
Config path used

/etc/apache2/sites-available/bcf-vm.conf



7.4.8 Debug logging too noisy (Apache or Shiny)
Symptom
Lots of LDAP debug output in logs during normal use.
Fix (Apache)
In mpiseq2/ngs-vm_01-main.conf, use normal logging:
LogLevel warn
# LogLevel authnz_ldap:debug ldap:debug   # enable only when debugging
Fix (Shiny app)
The app prints LDAP debug logs when:

LDAP_DEBUG=1

So for production:

Remove LDAP_DEBUG=1 from /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/.Renviron (or set it to 0).
Restart Shiny:

sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
If I need debugging later, I can temporarily set:
LDAP_DEBUG=1
and restart the service again.


7.4.9 LDAP mode configured, but app still shows local page
Symptom

Apache LDAP prompt appears and credentials are accepted.
App opens, but shows local login / local-mode content.

Cause
AUTH_MODE=ldap exists in systemd override, but those env vars are not reliably visible inside the Shiny R worker.
Fix
Set LDAP env in app-local .Renviron (runtime source used by R):
sudo tee /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/.Renviron >/dev/null <<'EOF'
AUTH_MODE=ldap
LDAP_URI=ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv2.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv3.biochem.mpg.de:636
LDAP_BASE_DN=dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de
TRUST_PROXY_AUTH_USER_QUERY=1
LDAP_DEBUG=1
EOF
sudo chown shiny:shiny /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/.Renviron
sudo chmod 640 /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/.Renviron
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
Verify in Shiny log:
LATEST=$(sudo ls -1t /var/log/shiny-server/sequencing-app-shiny-*.log | head -n1)
sudo tail -n 120 "$LATEST"
Expected:

LDAP DEBUG: startup env AUTH_MODE= ldap ...

After confirmation, remove LDAP_DEBUG=1 and restart again.


7.4.10 LDAP DEBUG ERROR (onFlushed): Operation not allowed without an active reactive context
Meaning

This is a debug-only logging issue, not an LDAP login failure.

Action

Safe to ignore while LDAP_DEBUG=1 is enabled.
Disable debug in production (LDAP_DEBUG unset) to stop this message.

title: 7  VM Troubleshooting Guide
text:
7.5 ngs-vm Cutover (443/9443)

7.5.1 Main URL still serves Perl after cutover
Check active vhosts:
sudo apache2ctl -S
Fix:
sudo a2ensite ngs-vm-shiny-443.conf
sudo a2dissite 02-testing.conf || true
sudo a2dissite default-ssl.conf || true
sudo apache2ctl -t

Or run the scripted cutover:
cd /home/yeroslaviz/BCFNGS-project-management
./scripts/cutover_ngs_vm_ports.sh cutover


7.5.2 Perl fallback on :9443 returns connection refused
Common causes:

Listen 9443 missing in /etc/apache2/ports.conf
fallback site not enabled

Fix:
grep -q '^Listen 9443$' /etc/apache2/ports.conf || echo 'Listen 9443' | sudo tee -a /etc/apache2/ports.conf
sudo a2ensite ngs-vm-perl-9443.conf
sudo apache2ctl -t
sudo systemctl restart apache2


7.5.3 LDAP login works on :8443 but fails on no-port :443
Cause:

ngs-vm-shiny-443.conf is missing LDAP <Location /sequencing-app/> auth block or is not the active :443 vhost.

Fix:

Copy from repo template and re-enable:

sudo cp /home/yeroslaviz/BCFNGS-project-management/scripts/ngs-vm-shiny-443.conf /etc/apache2/sites-available/ngs-vm-shiny-443.conf
sudo a2ensite ngs-vm-shiny-443.conf
sudo apache2ctl -t
sudo systemctl restart apache2

text:
7.6 SQL (moved)
All SQL database commands and explanations are documented in the SQL Handling chapter (Documentation/chapters/sql-handling.qmd).
This includes admin promotion in LDAP mode, SQLite inspection commands, budget holder updates, and the legacy import workflow.

7.6.1 SQLite: “attempt to write a readonly database”
If you see sqlite3.OperationalError: attempt to write a readonly database when running setup/import scripts, you are usually running as the wrong user.

On the VM, the live DB under /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/ is typically owned by shiny.
Stop Shiny Server, run the command as shiny, then start it again:

sudo systemctl stop shiny-server
sudo -u shiny <your command here>
sudo systemctl start shiny-server
If it still fails, check DB ownership/permissions:
ls -l /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db

7.7 Auth mode switching
LDAP mode (preferred: app .Renviron)
sudo sed -i 's/^AUTH_MODE=.*/AUTH_MODE=ldap/' /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/.Renviron
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
Local mode (preferred: app .Renviron)
sudo sed -i 's/^AUTH_MODE=.*/AUTH_MODE=local/' /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/.Renviron
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
Alternative (systemd override) is still valid:
sudo systemctl edit shiny-server
Then set Environment=AUTH_MODE=... and restart.

title: 7  VM Troubleshooting Guide
text:
7.14 Troubleshooting by symptom

7.14.1 ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS
Likely cause:

Apache rewrite rule loop in /etc/apache2/sites-enabled/01-main.conf.

Fix:
sudo cp ~/BCFNGS-project-management/mpiseq2/ngs-vm_01-main.conf /etc/apache2/sites-available/01-main.conf
sudo ln -sf /etc/apache2/sites-available/01-main.conf /etc/apache2/sites-enabled/01-main.conf
sudo apache2ctl configtest
sudo systemctl restart apache2
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
Then re-run curl checks from section 4.


7.14.2 App shows local login page + LDAP mode enabled but no authenticated user found
Likely cause:

Shiny is in LDAP mode, but proxy trust/query fallback settings are missing.

Checks:
sudo systemctl show shiny-server --property=Environment
Required:

AUTH_MODE=ldap
TRUST_PROXY_AUTH_USER_QUERY=1



7.14.3 Disconnected from the server. Reload
Likely causes:

Shiny worker restarted/terminated
Apache websocket/proxy path mismatch

Checks:
sudo journalctl -u shiny-server -n 200 --no-pager
sudo tail -n 200 /var/log/shiny-server/$(sudo ls -t /var/log/shiny-server | head -n 1)
sudo tail -n 200 /var/log/apache2/sequencing-app-ssl-error.log
Also ensure Apache modules are enabled:
sudo apache2ctl -M | egrep 'headers_module|rewrite_module|proxy_module|proxy_http_module|proxy_wstunnel_module|ldap_module|authnz_ldap_module'


7.14.4 User can impersonate another user via URL query
Expected fixed behavior:

tampered auth_user should always be rewritten to the authenticated LDAP username.

If not fixed, re-apply mpiseq2/ngs-vm_01-main.conf from repo and restart Apache + Shiny (section 5A).

title: 7  VM Troubleshooting Guide
text:
7.15 Certificate warning note
If Apache restart shows:

AH01909: server certificate does NOT include an ID which matches the server name

This is a certificate/SAN mismatch warning for host naming. It is separate from LDAP auth logic and redirect rewrite behavior.

title: 7  VM Troubleshooting Guide
section: 7.17 Possible errors and fixes (VM LDAP rollout)
text:
7.17 Possible errors and fixes (VM LDAP rollout)
This section captures the real errors I hit during the VM rollout and the fixes that worked. It is meant as a troubleshooting reference.

7.17.1 Apache won’t restart after LDAP change
Symptom
AH00526: Syntax error ... Bad LDAP URL while parsing.
Cause
Apache’s AuthLDAPURL is very strict. Each URL must be complete (host + base DN + query), and some Apache builds don’t accept multiple URLs in a single directive.
Fix
Use a single server (most stable on my VM):
AuthLDAPURL "ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de:636/dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de?uid?sub?(objectClass=posixAccount)"
If the multi‑server line fails, I keep one server and fall back manually if needed.


7.17.2 LDAPTLS_CACERTDIR breaks Apache
Symptom
Invalid command 'LDAPTLS_CACERTDIR'
Cause
LDAPTLS_CACERTDIR is not an Apache directive. It belongs to the OpenLDAP client config.
Fix
Remove it from Apache. If I need TLS trust, I configure it in /etc/ldap/ldap.conf instead.


7.17.3 LDAP login shows 500 Internal Server Error
Symptom

Browser shows Internal Server Error
Apache log shows:

ldap_simple_bind() failed][Can't contact LDAP server]
Cause
LDAP TLS handshake fails (certificate trust not configured).
Fix

Confirm port reachability:

nc -vz ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de 636

Test LDAP with TLS bypass to confirm trust problem:

LDAPTLS_REQCERT=never ldapsearch -x -H ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de:636 \
  -b "dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de" "(uid=USER)" mail

Fix trust in /etc/ldap/ldap.conf:

TLS_CACERTDIR /etc/ssl/certs
Important: I had a typo (TLS_CACERETDIR) which prevented TLS trust from loading. Fixing the spelling made LDAP work.

Verify normal LDAP now works:

ldapsearch -x -H ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de:636 \
  -b "dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de" "(uid=USER)" mail
Then restart Apache:
sudo systemctl restart apache2


7.17.4 Shiny app runs locally but crashes via Apache
Symptom

App works with shiny::runApp()
But Apache shows 500 and Shiny worker terminates

Cause

title: 7  VM Troubleshooting Guide
text:
7.18 Vibe Coding

7.18.1 AI session restart (quick resume)
If I need to resume work in a new AI session, I point it to:

Quick Start — how to run locally/VM, and environment variables.
Setup & Infrastructure — system setup, Apache/Shiny layout, base assumptions.
Deploy & Operations — rollout steps, backups, and validation checks.
LDAP Authentication — proxy/LDAP flow and expected headers.
SQL Handling — DB inspection, rebuild, and admin/user updates.
Change Log — latest decisions and what changed recently.

LDAP authentication failure in Apache (not an app crash).
Fix
Check Apache log for authnz_ldap errors and fix TLS trust as above.

title: 7  VM Troubleshooting Guide
text:
7.19 Comprehensive Error‑Checking Checklist
When something breaks, I run through this list in order:

Apache syntax

sudo apache2ctl -t

Which vhost is active

sudo apache2ctl -S

Proxy path

sudo grep -n "ProxyPass" -R /etc/apache2/sites-enabled /etc/apache2/conf-enabled

LDAP connectivity + TLS

nc -vz ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de 636
ldapsearch -x -H ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de:636 \
  -b "dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de" "(uid=USER)" mail
If that fails, verify /etc/ldap/ldap.conf has:
TLS_CACERTDIR /etc/ssl/certs

LDAP auth prompt vs app redirect

curl -Ik https://HOST/
curl -Ik -u USER https://HOST/
Expected: 401 without user, 302 to /sequencing-app/?auth_user=USER with user.

Shiny backend is reachable

curl -I http://localhost:3838/sequencing-app/

Apache app path

curl -Ik -u USER https://HOST/sequencing-app/

Shiny logs

sudo ls -t /var/log/shiny-server/ | head -n 1
sudo tail -n 200 /var/log/shiny-server/FILE.log

Shiny environment variables

sudo systemctl show shiny-server --property=Environment

Database permissions

ls -l /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db

Reload after config change

sudo systemctl restart apache2
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server

title: 8  Change Log
text:
8.2 Changes on 03.03.2026

Updated admin cost-notification recipient routing:

replaced project creator (projects.user_id) email with responsible user email resolution.
resolution order now supports:

username match in users
unique full-name match in users
direct email stored in responsible_user (legacy/manual fallback).

recipient set for admin cost-notification remains:

budget holder email
resolved responsible user email
ngs@biochem.mpg.de.


Fixed responsible-user normalization crash on larger user tables:

normalize_key() is now vector-safe and no longer applies scalar %||% logic to full vectors.
resolves runtime error: length = <n> in coercion to logical(1) seen in project table rendering and responsible-user migration flow.

Standardized user-facing terminology:

changed Service Type(s) to Sample Service Type(s) across the app UI, notifications, and source docs.
database schema/table names remain unchanged (service_types, service_type_id).

Updated admin cost-estimation HTML email formatting:

added Sequencing Platform line to the email details payload/body.
both lines are now emphasized in red:

Sample Service Type: ...
Sequencing Platform: ....


Added compatibility rewrite for project-creation email templates:

if an existing DB template still contains - Service Type:, the send path rewrites it to - Sample Service Type: at runtime.

title: 8  Change Log
text:
8.3 Changes on 02.03.2026

Reworked responsible-user handling in sequencing-app/app.R:

added canonicalization helpers so new/edited projects store stable username values in projects.responsible_user.
kept legacy compatibility for historical entries (full-name only and label-like values).

Updated responsible-user dropdown behavior in Create/Edit project dialogs:

choices now use named values (label -> username).
label format is Full Name (username) when full name is available.
choices are sorted alphabetically and duplicate full names are disambiguated by username.
edit modal preserves unresolved legacy values via a single Legacy entry: ... option.

Added startup normalization/migration for legacy responsible-user values:

exact matches are auto-normalized to canonical username.
unresolved values are reported and not overwritten.

Updated permissions and project visibility checks to support mixed historical formats:

canonical username
legacy full name
label format Full Name (username).

Updated display and notifications:

projects table now renders responsible users in friendly format (Full Name (username) where resolvable).
project creation and cost-notification emails now use the same friendly responsible-user display.

Fixed startup error on larger user tables (length = 78 in coercion to logical(1)):

hardened scalar coercion in responsible-user helper functions.
wrapped legacy normalization at startup in tryCatch to avoid blocking app startup if migration encounters unexpected data.

Validated the feature on bcf-vm after deploy from the feature branch; dropdown behavior and responsible-user flow worked as expected.

title: 8  Change Log

Added support for admin-managed additional project costs:

new DB column projects.additional_cost

sends cost-estimation mail to project creator + budget holder + ngs@biochem.mpg.de.

cost line is bold + underlined and includes formatted value (e.g. 2460.00€).

Total Project Costs will be provided soon after project assessment by the NGS core facility.


Documentation updates:

title: 8  Change Log
text:

8.12.1 VM rollout validation (what I tested and confirmed)
I verified end‑to‑end behavior on the VM after fixing the Apache proxy path:

LDAP login works: the browser prompts for username/password and redirects to /sequencing-app/?auth_user=<user>.
User mapping works: yeroslaviz (OU contains Cox) is mapped to the Cox group and the correct PI.
User mapping works: yeroslaviz-test (OU = NGS Facility) is treated as a service‑group user (no PI match).
Email routing works: research group users trigger mail to the PI and NGS.
Email routing works: service group users (no PI match) trigger mail to the administrator.

If all three happen, the LDAP→app→email flow is working as intended.

title: 8  Change Log
text:
8.13 Changes on 06.02.2026

Added LDAP login flow in the Shiny app (auto‑login via X-Remote-User, local dev simulation, and optional attribute lookup).

Added dev tools toggle (dev only) to switch between LDAP and local auth while testing.
Mapped LDAP ou values to budget holders, auto‑selecting the correct PI in the project form (with partial match fallback).
Added budget holder notices for multiple cost centers and missing cost center values.

Updated scripts/deploy.sh to use rsync and exclude sequencing_projects.db.
Updated Apache LDAP configuration template and added local dev testing instructions + env var cleanup.
I do not track SQLite DB files in git because they change constantly, are environment‑specific (local vs VM), and can contain sensitive data. I ignore *.db/*.sqlite and keep only the schema/seed files in git.
